title: The One With The Baby Shower

jokes.text: What? You mean they're not coming to a social event where there's no men and no booze? That's shocking.
jokes.setup: Rachel's sisters called to say they can't make the baby shower
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic surprise that they'd skip an event with no men or alcohol
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcastic observation about Rachel's shallow sisters - economical and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm shocked. Shocked! My sisters are skipping an event with no men and no alcohol? How is that even possible?

jokes.text: Well, given that we forgot to invite her... it would be an awfully big coincidence if she was.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks if her mom will be at the shower
jokes.punchline: Dry delivery revealing they forgot to invite her entirely
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of an uncomfortable truth - Monica's neurotic organization failing spectacularly

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Will my mom be at the shower?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: About that...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We may have forgotten to invite her.

jokes.text: Well, I don't have a mother. So often I forget other people--
jokes.setup: Phoebe needs to explain why she forgot to invite Rachel's mother
jokes.punchline: Classic Phoebe logic - her lack of mother makes her forget others have them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe character moment - her tragic backstory used as illogical excuse with complete sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I don't have a mother, so I forget other people have them.

jokes.text: Oh, give it a rest.
jokes.setup: Phoebe starts her typical tragic backstory excuse
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt dismissal cuts through Phoebe's routine
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Monica's impatience with Phoebe's habitual victim narrative

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, when I was a kid, my mother used to lock me in the closet...

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, we've heard this one. Can you just eat your sandwich?

jokes.text: Wait a minute. If you're in charge of invitations, why am I calling her?
jokes.setup: Monica has to call Rachel's mother to invite her
jokes.punchline: Realizes the logical flaw mid-task
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about task delegation logic

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Hi Mrs. Green? It's Monica Geller. I'm calling to invite you to... oh my God, what am I doing? You're Rachel's mother. Why am I calling you to invite you to something for Rachel?

jokes.text: I know. My daughters told me about it when they received their impromptu invitations a month ago.
jokes.setup: Monica claims the shower is 'impromptu'
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Green reveals the lie - others got invitations a month ago
jokes.explanation: Perfectly structured trap - Mrs. Green catches Monica in her lie with surgical precision

dialogue.speaker: Monica Geller
dialogue.line: It's just an impromptu little shower.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Impromptu? Monica, I've had this invitation for a month!

jokes.text: For not inviting me, or for lying about it?
jokes.setup: Monica apologizes profusely
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Green clarifies there are multiple offenses to apologize for
jokes.explanation: Brutal escalation - Mrs. Green making Monica squirm by itemizing her failures

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mrs. Green, I am so sorry!

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Oh honey, you're going to have to be more specific. Which thing are you apologizing for?

jokes.text: Oh, my God. My ass is sweating.
jokes.setup: Monica is caught in multiple lies by Mrs. Green
jokes.punchline: Crude physical manifestation of her extreme anxiety
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Monica's neurotic anxiety with a memorably crude confession

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh God, oh God, oh God!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can explain everything!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Green
dialogue.line: Can you explain why you told me you were an only child?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That was... that was just a joke!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Green
dialogue.line: And that you've never been to Long Island?

jokes.text: Isn't it at 3? - Son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Monica tells Mrs. Green the shower is at 4pm
jokes.punchline: Phoebe reminds her it's actually at 3, Monica curses
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - just when Monica thinks she's recovered, another mistake is revealed

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Actually, it's at 3.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, crap!

jokes.text: You'll be perfect for this. That's already your name.
jokes.setup: Joey explains he'll be Dr. Drake Ramoray by day, Joey Tribbiani by night
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out the obvious - Joey Tribbiani is already his name
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - deflating Joey's dramatic announcement with simple logic

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey Tribbiani is already your name.

jokes.text: Okay, I guess we can lose to junior-high girls some other time.
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler were going to play basketball
jokes.punchline: Chandler implies they'd lose even to junior high girls
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor about their athletic abilities

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're not going to win. We're going to get crushed by a bunch of junior high girls.

jokes.text: Bamboozled? - Isn't that a cool name? - Yeah. - No.
jokes.setup: Joey announces the game name 'Bamboozled'
jokes.punchline: Ross and Chandler have opposite reactions
jokes.explanation: Simple character contrast - Ross is supportive, Chandler is skeptical

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Bamboozled!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, come on!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I love this game!

jokes.text: Well, Joey, I'm a headhunter. I hook up out-of-work Soviet scientists with rogue Third World nations. Hi, Rasputin.
jokes.setup: Joey asks Chandler to introduce himself for the game show practice
jokes.punchline: Chandler creates an absurd spy movie backstory instead of his real job
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Chandler moment - turning mundane game show intro into elaborate spy fiction with 'Hi, Rasputin' as perfect absurd detail

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Come on, introduce yourself for the game show practice.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Chandler Bing. I'm a statistical analysis and data reconfiguration... No wait, that's boring. I'm actually a retired spy. I've infiltrated 47 countries, speak 12 languages, and once saved the world with nothing but a paperclip and my charm.

jokes.text: It's Bogoda, but close enough.
jokes.setup: Chandler correctly answers 'Bogotá' as capital of Colombia
jokes.punchline: Joey mispronounces it as 'Bogoda' while being wrong about being right
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey stupidity - confidently correcting the right answer with a wrong pronunciation

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Bogoda!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, it's Bogotá.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's what I said, Bogoda!

jokes.text: Higher or lower than what? - This is embarrassing.
jokes.setup: Joey explains a Wicked Wango Card determines if you go higher or lower
jokes.punchline: Chandler asks the obvious follow-up question Joey can't answer
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Joey's confidence crumbling when faced with basic logic

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: The Wicked Wango Card determines if you go higher or lower.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What's a Wicked Wango Card?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: ...I don't know.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I don't believe contestants are allowed to talk to each other.
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler are mocking the game to each other
jokes.punchline: Joey tries to maintain game show authority despite his incompetence
jokes.explanation: Joey desperately clinging to game show host dignity while clearly failing

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, settle down everyone. I am the host of this game show and I will be asking the questions.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Joey, you don't even know what the game is.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That doesn't matter! I have the card, I have the suit, and I have this very authoritative voice.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You sound like you're selling paper towels.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I do NOT! And for that comment, you lose a turn.

jokes.text: Why? He's gonna be dressed as a baby.
jokes.setup: Monica says ordering a stripper for the shower is inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Phoebe thinks baby costume makes it appropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Phoebe logic - baby costume somehow makes stripper appropriate for baby shower

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Ordering a stripper for the shower is inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What if she wore a baby costume?

jokes.text: Try.
jokes.setup: Monica and Phoebe say they couldn't feel worse about forgetting to invite her
jokes.punchline: Single word challenge to feel even worse
jokes.explanation: Devastating single-word comeback. Perfect economy and delivery - the ultimate 'hold my beer' response

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We feel terrible about forgetting to invite you.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, we couldn't feel worse.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Wanna bet?

jokes.text: Isn't it great? One less person we have to make small talk with.
jokes.setup: Monica notes that Sandra is still mad at them
jokes.punchline: Phoebe sees the upside - less social obligation
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - finding the silver lining in social awkwardness with complete sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Sandra is still mad at us.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Well, that's good! Now we don't have to invite her to stuff!

jokes.text: I gotta go powder my ass.
jokes.setup: Monica needs to use the bathroom but wants to sound polite
jokes.punchline: Accidentally combines 'powder my nose' with her earlier 'ass sweating' comment
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback mixing polite euphemism with Monica's earlier crude anxiety confession

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm just going to go powder my ass.

jokes.text: Look at that face. Just like when you were in high school. If I didn't know better, I'd say you were a cheerleader in trouble.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Green observes Rachel's worried expression
jokes.punchline: Compares her to a 'cheerleader in trouble' - pregnancy euphemism
jokes.explanation: Subtle humor about Rachel's privileged background and current situation parallels

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: You look like a cheerleader in trouble.

jokes.text: It's like you're a cave person.
jokes.setup: Rachel says she won't use a nanny or housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Green compares this to primitive living
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mrs. Green's privileged perspective

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I won't use a nanny or housekeeper.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Oh honey, that's like living in the Dark Ages. You might as well be churning butter and carrying water from a well.

jokes.text: I actually think I remember some of it: [SPEAKS IN SPANISH]
jokes.setup: Rachel mentions Mrs. K taught her Spanish
jokes.punchline: She attempts to speak Spanish (presumably poorly based on reactions)
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of Rachel's poor Spanish - modest humor

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Mrs. K taught me Spanish.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: ¿Cómo estás?

jokes.text: I just had the greatest idea. I'm gonna come live with you.
jokes.setup: Rachel says she can't afford full-time help
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Green volunteers herself as the solution
jokes.explanation: Setup for extended storyline - Mrs. Green's 'helpful' offer creates new problems

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't afford full-time help.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Well, I'm free. I could help you out.

jokes.text: Aw, look at you. You have tears in your eyes. - Yes, yes, I do.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Green thinks Rachel is crying from joy
jokes.punchline: Rachel confirms she's crying - but clearly from horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect misread situation - tears of joy vs tears of horror with great dramatic irony

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Oh honey, are you crying? Happy tears?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yes... tears. From joy.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: That's wonderful!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, actually, these are terror tears.

jokes.text: No, dude, you gotta hold your breath till you're ready to answer the question. It's part of the game.
jokes.setup: Joey tells Ross to hold his breath for the question
jokes.punchline: Reveals this arbitrary rule is somehow part of the game
jokes.explanation: Absurd game show rule that makes no sense - typical Joey logic

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Okay, okay. Now hold your breath.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Because those are the rules.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What rules? You just made that up!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, now it's a rule.

jokes.text: This is ridiculous. He's not gonna hold-- [Ross starts holding his breath]
jokes.setup: Chandler dismisses the breath-holding rule
jokes.punchline: Ross actually starts holding his breath mid-protest
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - Ross getting caught up in the absurd game despite himself

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's ridiculous. Holding your breath doesn't work.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What are you talking about? Of course it works! When you hold your breath, the other person can't—

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Proving my point.

jokes.text: Fear of Triscuits. - No. No. Fear of the number 13. - Fear of Triscuits?
jokes.setup: Joey asks about 'triskaidekaphobia'
jokes.punchline: Ross mishears as 'Triscuits' and doubles down on the wrong answer
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with Ross's academic mind creating a plausible but wrong connection

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What's triskaidekaphobia?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, that's when you're afraid of Triscuits.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, it's the fear of the number thirteen.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Right, right, Triscuits. I know what I said.

jokes.text: It's possible. They have really sharp edges.
jokes.setup: Ross suggested 'fear of Triscuits' as the phobia meaning
jokes.punchline: He seriously defends this ridiculous answer with logical reasoning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Ross applying his academic reasoning to defend an absurd answer with complete seriousness

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no, it makes perfect sense! 'Trisca' — fear of, 'uits' — Triscuits. It's a perfectly valid phobia!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Ross, that's not how words work.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, and I suppose you have a better explanation?

jokes.text: Oh, come on, Ross. I think we're all losers here.
jokes.setup: Joey says Chandler is upset because he's losing
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out everyone is losing by playing this terrible game
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the absurdity of the situation - Chandler's perspective on the whole endeavor

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Chandler is upset because he's losing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Everyone is losing by playing this terrible game.

jokes.text: Choose, you jackass.
jokes.setup: Chandler keeps saying he doesn't care which option he picks
jokes.punchline: Joey breaks character with frustration and insults him
jokes.explanation: Joey's game show host facade cracking under pressure - good character moment

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I don't care which one we pick, they're both fine.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Dude, you've said that like five times! Just pick one!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I literally don't care.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Chandler, you're the most indecisive person I've ever met! How do you even get dressed in the morning?

jokes.text: This game is kind of fun. - You don't think it's a little crazy that you get all my points--? - I don't think the contestants are supposed to speak to each other.
jokes.setup: Chandler suddenly likes the game after winning Ross's points
jokes.punchline: He hypocritically shuts down Ross using Joey's earlier rule
jokes.explanation: Perfect hypocrisy - Chandler using the rule he mocked when it benefits him

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You know what? I'm actually really good at this game. I like this game now.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh come on, Chandler, you can't just start liking it because you're winning.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No points for you!

jokes.text: She can't hear you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe loudly declares she'd be honored if Mrs. Green lived with her
jokes.punchline: Monica points out Mrs. Green is out of earshot
jokes.explanation: Simple deflation of Phoebe's performative kindness

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She can't hear you. She's in the other room.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. She's gonna wanna sleep in my bed with me. This cannot happen.
jokes.setup: Rachel realizes her mother wants to sleep on the couch
jokes.punchline: Escalates to imagining her mother in her bed
jokes.explanation: Rachel's anxiety spiraling to worst-case scenario

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, you want to sleep on the couch?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, what if you want to sleep in my bed?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What if you want to sleep in my bed with me?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, what if you never leave?

jokes.text: And I'm just gonna swoop in there and be like the daughter she never had.
jokes.setup: Monica encourages Rachel to reject her mother's help
jokes.punchline: Reveals her ulterior motive to replace Rachel as favorite
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitiveness - scheming to become Mrs. Green's replacement daughter

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You should tell your mother you don't need her help. You need to be independent.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really? I thought you liked my mom.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I do! But... okay, fine. If she's not around helping you all the time, maybe she'll have more time to help me. And then I'll be her favorite.

jokes.text: I have new respect for Chandler.
jokes.setup: Phoebe witnesses Monica's manipulative scheme
jokes.punchline: Compares Monica's scheming to Chandler's earlier game manipulation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback connecting Monica's scheming to Chandler's game tactics

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, Monica, you are SO manipulative! You're like Chandler when he plays mind games!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I am not!

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yes, you are! You totally just pulled a Chandler Bing on us!

jokes.text: Well, um, I don't have a gift because I wasn't invited until the last minute. But thank you so much, dear, for bringing that to everybody's attention.
jokes.setup: Monica declares Mrs. Green the most important person and should open first gift
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Green sarcastically thanks her for highlighting her exclusion
jokes.explanation: Mrs. Green expertly turning Monica's attempt to make up into another embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're the most important person here, so you should open the first gift.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Oh, well thank you for pointing out that I've been excluded from everything else.

jokes.text: How about you less important people? Let's open your presents.
jokes.setup: Monica called Mrs. Green the most important person
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Green sarcastically refers to everyone else as 'less important'
jokes.explanation: Mrs. Green continuing to weaponize Monica's words against everyone

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're the most important person.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Oh, so everyone else is less important?

jokes.text: Really? Remember Twinkles? - He was a hamster. I'm not gonna vacuum up my baby.
jokes.setup: Rachel insists she can handle motherhood, Mrs. Green brings up 'Twinkles'
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals she accidentally vacuumed up a hamster but thinks babies are different
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reveal - casual mention of vacuuming up a hamster with Rachel's confident logic about size differences. Perfectly absurd and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can totally handle a baby. Babies are completely different from hamsters.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Really? Because I seem to recall a certain incident with 'Twinkles.'

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That was an accident! I didn't see him in the pile of laundry. How was I supposed to know he was in there before I turned on the vacuum?

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Exactly my point.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: But a baby won't fit in the vacuum, so...

jokes.text: Is that a beer bong for a baby?
jokes.setup: Rachel looks at a baby gift
jokes.punchline: Mistakes a breast pump for a beer bong
jokes.explanation: Rachel's party background showing - seeing baby equipment through party lens

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What is this? A beer bong?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's a breast pump, Rachel.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I was about to say, that seems like a lot of work for a beer.

jokes.text: Did I say I was done guessing? Okay, thank you for that.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Green corrects Rachel that it's a breast pump
jokes.punchline: Rachel tries to save face after embarrassing guess
jokes.explanation: Rachel's awkward attempt to recover from embarrassing mistake

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, a breast pump! Of course! I knew that. I was just... testing you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You know, to make sure you knew what it was. Because that's the kind of friend I am.

jokes.text: Well, that's gross. Why don't you just take it outside and throw it in a Dumpster?
jokes.setup: Learning the Diaper Genie stores dirty diapers
jokes.punchline: Rachel suggests throwing each diaper in a dumpster instead
jokes.explanation: Rachel's privileged mindset - hasn't considered practical aspects of baby care

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, so it just... keeps them in there? All of them?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's airtight, so you don't smell them.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Why would you do that? Why wouldn't you just throw them in a dumpster?

jokes.text: You're gonna do that 10 times a day? - What, it goes 10 times a day?
jokes.setup: Monica questions Rachel's dumpster plan
jokes.punchline: Rachel is shocked by frequency of diaper changes
jokes.explanation: Rachel's continued ignorance about basic baby care escalating

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're going to throw the baby in the dumpster?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? No! The diapers!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I had no idea you had to change them that often!

jokes.text: What are we feeding this baby, Indian food?
jokes.setup: Learning babies go through 10 diapers a day
jokes.punchline: Attributes frequency to spicy food rather than baby biology
jokes.explanation: Rachel applying adult logic to baby physiology - mild cultural stereotype but fits character

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ten diapers a day? That's so many! I thought babies only went to the bathroom like, twice a day.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, it's ten times a day. Babies have very fast metabolisms.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Well, that must be because of all the spicy food they eat.

jokes.text: I don't know, I'd leave it on the changing table? [ALL GASP]
jokes.setup: Asked what she'd do with baby while taking diapers to garbage
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dangerous suggestion horrifies everyone
jokes.explanation: Peak of Rachel's ignorance - suggesting something genuinely dangerous that horrifies experienced parents

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I could leave her in the car.

dialogue.speaker: Everyone
dialogue.line: *horrified gasps*

jokes.text: Of course, I know that. I mean, of course you never leave a baby alone. Ha, ha. I mean, it would-- She wouldn't be safe. You know, not as safe as she would be with me, the baby dummy. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Everyone gasps at Rachel's suggestion to leave baby alone
jokes.punchline: Rachel's panicked overcorrection and self-deprecating 'baby dummy' comment
jokes.explanation: Rachel's verbal flailing after making a mistake - realistic cringe but a bit lengthy

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? No, no, no! I didn't mean leave the baby alone alone. I just meant like, you know, give the baby some space to, like, develop its own personality.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I'm such a baby dummy.

jokes.text: It's actually a bassinet. - Okay, Mommy, don't ever leave me.
jokes.setup: Rachel calls a bassinet a beautiful basket
jokes.punchline: After correction, Rachel desperately clings to her mother
jokes.explanation: Rachel's confidence completely shattered - reverting to child-like dependence on mother

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Rachel, that's a bassinet.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I knew that. I was just... testing you.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Mom? MOM?! I need you!

jokes.text: [MIMICS MONKEY GIBBERING] - That noise can only mean one thing. - Hungry Monkey.
jokes.setup: Joey makes monkey noises during the game
jokes.punchline: Ross and Chandler somehow know this means 'Hungry Monkey'
jokes.explanation: Absurd game show element that Ross and Chandler have somehow learned - good escalation of the game's ridiculousness

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ooh ooh ooh! Eee eee eee!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, he's doing 'Hungry Monkey' again.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, we know.

jokes.text: But you forgot to switch legs between questions. So no Hopping Bonus. - Oh! Every time.
jokes.setup: Chandler correctly answers questions while hopping
jokes.punchline: Loses bonus for not switching legs - he's frustrated this always happens
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Chandler's 'Every time' suggests he's played this ridiculous game before and keeps making the same mistake

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, you got all the answers right? That's amazing!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, right? I'm on a roll!

dialogue.speaker: Host
dialogue.line: Congratulations! You've won the bonus round! Now, for an extra $10,000, you must switch legs and answer one more question.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Switch legs? But I'm on this leg!

dialogue.speaker: Host
dialogue.line: Those are the rules. Switch legs or forfeit the bonus.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: This always happens to me! Why do they always make me switch legs?!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Just switch, man. Come on!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Fine, fine... *switches legs* See? Now I'm gonna lose.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Congratulations, Ross. Because, Chandler... you've been Bamboozled. - No! - Yes! - This is the best game ever!
jokes.setup: Chandler picks a card in the game
jokes.punchline: Gets 'Bamboozled' and loses everything, his attitude completely changes
jokes.explanation: Great character flip - Chandler goes from loving the game to hating it instantly based on results

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm picking a card. This is it. This is my card.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Alright, you got 'Bamboozled.'

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? No, no, no! That's not... I lose everything?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, man, those are the rules.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, this is the worst day of my life. I'm done. I'm moving to Yemen.

jokes.text: Oh, can I throw up in my Diaper Genie?
jokes.setup: Rachel is overwhelmed about not knowing baby care
jokes.punchline: Wants to throw up in the diaper disposal system
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier gift confusion - Rachel still doesn't understand baby products

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I don't know anything about babies! What if I do something wrong?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh God, I think I'm gonna be sick. Where's the diaper pail?

jokes.text: I just Bamboozled Chandler. - Which is not a sexual thing.
jokes.setup: Ross explains why he's sweaty to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Clarifies the game term isn't sexual
jokes.explanation: Meta joke acknowledging how 'Bamboozled' could sound - Ross being characteristically awkward

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I was playing basketball, okay? I got all sweaty from playing basketball.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, you were playing with balls?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, Phoebe, basketballs. It's a game. You bounce the ball, you throw it in the hoop.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, okay.

jokes.text: Well, that was a quick shower. - Not if you were here.
jokes.setup: Ross comments on the shower ending quickly
jokes.punchline: Rachel implies it felt long and tedious from inside
jokes.explanation: Simple contrast between outside and inside perspectives of the shower

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That shower ended pretty quickly.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, well it felt like forever in there.

jokes.text: A Play-Doh Barbershop? - No. She's going to live with us for eight weeks.
jokes.setup: Rachel says her mom got them the greatest gift
jokes.punchline: Ross misidentifies a toy as the gift, but it's actually Mrs. Green moving in
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Ross focuses on wrong thing while Rachel reveals the real 'gift'

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: My mom got us the greatest gift.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What is it?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A new coffee table!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh wow, that's nice.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, that's not it. My mom is moving in with us.

jokes.text: Hi, Ross. - Hey, roomie.
jokes.setup: Ross learns Mrs. Green is moving in
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Green immediately establishes familiarity by calling him 'roomie'
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment - Mrs. Green already acting like they're living together

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mom's moving in with us.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What? When?

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: Hello, roomie!

jokes.text: [IN EMBELLISHED VOICE] It's a pleasure to meet you, Ray.
jokes.setup: Joey meets the game show producer
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to his fake sophisticated voice
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey - trying to impress with his 'classy' voice for the audition

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey, how's it going? I'm Joey Tribbiani.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I'm an actor. I've been in a lot of TV shows and movies. You might have seen me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Ah, well, actually, I prefer the finer things in life. I'm quite the thespian, you know. My craft is very important to me, and I take my work very seriously indeed.

jokes.text: What's complicated? You spin the Wheel of Mayhem to go up the Ladder of Chance... past the Mud Hut through the Rainbow Ring to get to the Golden Monkey. You yank his tail, and boom, you're in Paradise Pond.
jokes.setup: Producer says the game was too complicated
jokes.punchline: Joey rattles off increasingly ridiculous game elements as if they're logical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist rant - Joey's complete commitment to nonsensical game elements delivered with total seriousness

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Too complicated? It's simple! You roll the dice, move your piece around the board, collect cards, build your empire, fight off the dragon, solve the riddle, find the hidden treasure, and whoever reaches Shangri-La first wins!

jokes.text: Well, what's fun about that? You expect me to be the host of a boring game that's just people standing around answering questions?
jokes.setup: Producer explains it's now just a simple question-answer game
jokes.punchline: Joey dismisses this as boring - which is what most game shows actually are
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about game show formats - Joey's critique applies to most real game shows

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: A question-answer game? That's boring. That's what all game shows are!

jokes.text: Well, there'll be women in bikinis holding up the scores. - [IN EMBELLISHED VOICE] Let's play Bamboozled.
jokes.setup: Producer mentions bikini-clad women as consolation
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately switches back to his game show host voice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - completely changes mind when shallow appeal is mentioned

dialogue.speaker: Producer
dialogue.line: Hey, look on the bright side. We can get you bikini-clad women to jump around on stage.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Really? When do we start?

jokes.text: Did I say garage? I meant garbage.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Green suggests Ross's dinosaur stuff belongs in the garage
jokes.punchline: Corrects herself to say garbage instead
jokes.explanation: Brutal escalation delivered with perfect deadpan - Mrs. Green's 'correction' is devastating

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: All this dinosaur stuff should go in the garage.

dialogue.speaker: Sandra Green
dialogue.line: I mean, the garbage.

jokes.text: With another woman. - Have you no control, Ross?
jokes.setup: Ross mentions his son with another mother
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Green implies he's promiscuous and lacks self-control
jokes.explanation: Mrs. Green making Ross's reasonable explanation sound scandalous

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I have a son with Carol.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Green
dialogue.line: A son? Ross, how many women are you with?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, Carol is my ex-wife. We had a son together before we got divorced.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Green
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, that's still one too many.

jokes.text: Hello? I still don't know what the hell I'm doing.
jokes.setup: Ross and Mrs. Green work out that they don't need her help
jokes.punchline: Rachel interrupts to remind them she's still clueless
jokes.explanation: Rachel asserting she's still the problem that needs solving

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm still here, and I still have no idea what's going on.

jokes.text: Nothing. You have apologized to her like a million times. And she's been nothing but terrible to you. And don't forget, you just threw her daughter a lovely, albeit slightly boring, shower.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks what to do about Mrs. Green being mad
jokes.punchline: Monica lists reasons why Phoebe shouldn't feel guilty, including backhanded shower compliment
jokes.explanation: Monica's support with a buried insult about the boring shower

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: What should I do about Mrs. Green being mad at me?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, you shouldn't feel guilty. She's been mean to you for years. Remember when she said your shower curtain was nice, but then asked if you got it at a yard sale?

jokes.text: Maybe it's time you took a good, hard look in the mirror, young lady. - Old lady. - Lady.
jokes.setup: Monica tells off Mrs. Green
jokes.punchline: Realizes 'young lady' is wrong, overcorrects to 'old lady', then settles on 'lady'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica neurosis - ruining her big moment by overthinking word choice mid-rant

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what, young lady—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Wait, no, old lady—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Lady.

jokes.text: Wrap it up. Wrap it up. Wrap it up.
jokes.setup: Monica is stumbling through her confrontation with Mrs. Green
jokes.punchline: Phoebe urgently tells her to end it before it gets worse
jokes.explanation: Phoebe recognizing Monica's confrontation is going off the rails

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Monica, just end it! End it now before it gets worse!

jokes.text: I can't feel my legs.
jokes.setup: Monica just confronted Mrs. Green
jokes.punchline: Adrenaline rush leaves her physically affected
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of Monica's post-confrontation crash

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mrs. Green, I need to talk to you about what happened at Thanksgiving.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I can't believe you said those things about me in front of everyone!

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Green
dialogue.line: Monica, I was just being honest.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Honest?! You called me a control freak who can't let anyone else cook in her kitchen!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I can't... I can't feel my legs... Why can't I feel my legs?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Is anyone else hot? I'm sweating. I'm shaking. What is happening to me?

jokes.text: [THUDDING] - Okay. I bit my tongue. But I'm still really sorry.
jokes.setup: Monica tries to apologize to Mrs. Green again
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of her falling or running into something, then continuing apology
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Monica's determination to apologize despite injury

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Mrs. Green, I'm so sorry about what happened at Thanksgiving. I feel terrible and I just want to—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: [falls/runs into something]

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: —apologize. As I was saying, I'm really, really sorry.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm sorry. You've been Bamboozled. You're gonna be a terrible mother.
jokes.setup: Ross is testing Rachel's baby knowledge using the game format
jokes.punchline: Uses the game's cruel elimination to deliver harsh verdict about her mothering
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark callback - using the absurd game format to deliver genuinely harsh judgment. Perfect integration of A and B plots.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Okay Rachel, this is the baby knowledge round. Question one: What do you do if your baby won't stop crying?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Um... sing to them?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, that's incorrect. You're eliminated from motherhood.

jokes.text: I've lost sight of why we're doing this.
jokes.setup: Ross declares her a terrible mother using game show format
jokes.punchline: Rachel questions the entire exercise
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where Rachel recognizes the absurdity - perfect episode ender

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Rachel Green, you are a terrible mother!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, what? That's not even a real thing. This is ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You can't just declare someone a terrible mother based on a game show format!

tmdb_overview: Monica and Phoebe throw Rachel a baby shower but forget to invite Rachel's mom. During the baby shower Rachel panics when she realizes she has no idea how to look after the baby after the birth. Meanwhile, Joey auditions for a job as a host for a new game show called "Bamboozled", and Chandler and Ross help him practice.
insight: Baby shower chaos sustains 2.8 jokes-per-minute through escalating cringe over 24 minutes.
